chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-415 of 2023 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) For orders on MA-1489/23 (urgency) For orders on office objection For orders on MA-1490/23 (exemption) For hearing of main case 25.09.2023 Mr. Muhammad Hassan Chang advocate for petitioner. --------- 1. Urgency granted. 2to4. Through this petition, the petitioner has prayed as under:- (a) To issue a writ of prohibition to the respondents No.7 to 9 from harassing the petitioner and his farmer and also restrain from causing any harm to the land / fields / crops / cultivation of the petitioner. (b) To issue writ of mandatory law, issuing an order to the official respondents to perform mandatory duties correctly and not to lodge any false FIR against the petitioner and his farmers at the behest of respondents No.7 to 9. (c ) To direct the respondents No.2 to 6 to provide legal protection of life, honour, prestige and property to the petitioner and his farmers from the cruel clutches of the respondents No.7 to 9 and treat them like respectable citizens as guaranteed in the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan. (d) Costs of the petition may be saddled upon the respondents. (e) Any other relief(s) which this Hon’ble Court deems fit, just and proper in favour of the petitioner. Record reflects that almost for the same relief, the petitioner has already approached to the Ex-Officio Justice of Peace and the same was allowed vide order dated 10.06.2023 with specific directions to the respondents not to illegally, unlawfully cause harassment to the petitioner in any manner and further respondents No.1 and 2 in the said application were directed to provide legal protection.

Through this petition, the petitioner has prayed as under:- (a) To issue a writ of prohibition to the respondents No.7 to 9 from harassing the petitioner and his farmer and also restrain from causing any harm to the land / fields / crops / cultivation of the petitioner. (b) To issue writ of mandatory law, issuing an order to the official respondents to perform mandatory duties correctly and not to lodge any false FIR against the petitioner and his farmers at the behest of respondents No.7 to 9. (c ) To direct the respondents No.2 to 6 to provide legal protection of life, honour, prestige and property to the petitioner and his farmers from the cruel clutches of the respondents No.7 to 9 and treat them like respectable citizens as guaranteed in the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan. (d) Costs of the petition may be saddled upon the respondents. (e) Any other relief(s) which this Hon’ble Court deems fit, just and proper in favour of the petitioner. Record reflects that almost for the same relief, the petitioner has already approached to the Ex-Officio Justice of Peace and the same was allowed vide order dated 10.06.2023 with specific directions to the respondents not to illegally, unlawfully cause harassment to the petitioner in any manner and further respondents No.1 and 2 in the said application were directed to provide legal protection. Inspite of his application for the relief claimed was entertained and allowed, the petitioner approached this Court for the same relief. In view of the above, this petition is not maintainable and dismissed in limine along with pending application(s) with cost of Rs.5000/-(rupees five thousand), which may be deposited by the petitioner in the account of dispensary of this Court within fifteen days. In case, the petitioner is not deposited the said amount within stipulated time, then the matter should be placed before the Court for further orders. g JUDGE
